seo.title: Fintech Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in fintech. Covers SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in fintech environments introduces significant regulatory exposure across SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and BSA/AML frameworks, where a single misconfigured extraction pipeline can trigger SEC enforcement actions or compromise cardholder data. Schema-guided LLM extraction from unstructured financial documents—such as bank statements, trade confirmations, and KYC files—demands rigorous validation at every layer to ensure field accuracy, completeness, and auditability. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your extraction agents meet the compliance bar required for critical financial infrastructure.

sections.heading: Data Ingestion & Classification Controls
sections.description: Establish strict controls over what data enters the extraction pipeline, ensuring PCI DSS scoping, GDPR lawful basis, and BSA/AML data handling requirements are met before any LLM processing begins.

items.label: Classify all input documents by data sensitivity tier
items.description: Tag each document type (e.g., bank statements, wire transfer records, KYC files) with a sensitivity classification (PII, PCI, AML-relevant) before ingestion so downstream controls are applied correctly.

items.label: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for all document ingestion endpoints
items.description: All document upload and transfer endpoints must use TLS 1.2 or higher to satisfy PCI DSS Requirement 4.2.1 for data in transit protection.

items.label: Implement AES-256 encryption for documents at rest
items.description: Store all ingested financial documents using AES-256 encryption at rest, with key management via a dedicated HSM or KMS, satisfying PCI DSS Requirement 3.5 and SOX data integrity controls.

items.label: Validate lawful basis for GDPR-covered documents before processing
items.description: For any document containing EU resident data, confirm and log the GDPR lawful basis (e.g., contract performance, legal obligation) prior to LLM extraction to avoid Article 6 violations.

items.label: Redact or tokenize PANs before LLM context windows
items.description: Use a pre-processing step to detect and tokenize Primary Account Numbers (PANs) and CVVs before passing document text to the LLM, ensuring cardholder data never enters model context in violation of PCI DSS Requirement 3.3.

items.label: Log all document ingestion events with immutable audit trail
items.description: Record document source, ingestion timestamp, user/system identity, and document hash to an append-only audit log to support SOX Section 302/404 audit trails and SEC examination readiness.

items.label: Enforce document retention and deletion policies at ingestion
items.description: Apply automated retention schedules at ingestion time based on document type (e.g., 7-year retention for SOX-relevant records, right-to-erasure flags for GDPR subjects) to prevent policy violations downstream.

sections.heading: Schema Validation & Extraction Accuracy
sections.description: Ensure that LLM-generated structured outputs conform to defined schemas, meet field-level accuracy requirements, and are validated for completeness before any downstream financial processing or reporting.

items.label: Define and version-control extraction schemas per document type
items.description: Maintain versioned JSON Schema or Pydantic models for each document type (e.g., trade confirmation schema v2.1) so extraction outputs are validated against a known, auditable contract.

items.label: Enforce strict schema validation on every LLM output before persistence
items.description: Reject and quarantine any extraction output that fails schema validation (missing required fields, wrong types, out-of-range values) rather than passing partial data to downstream systems.

items.label: Implement field-level confidence scoring for critical financial fields
items.description: For fields such as transaction amount, account number, and trade date, require the extraction pipeline to produce a confidence score and route low-confidence extractions to human review queues.

items.label: Validate numeric field types and ranges against business rules
items.description: Apply domain-specific range checks (e.g., transaction amounts must be positive, interest rates between 0–100%) as a post-extraction validation layer to catch LLM hallucinations before they affect financial records.

items.label: Run completeness checks against required field lists per document type
items.description: After extraction, verify that all mandatory fields defined in the schema are populated; flag incomplete records and prevent them from entering core banking or reporting systems.

items.label: Cross-validate extracted totals against document-level checksums where available
items.description: Where source documents include subtotals or grand totals (e.g., statement balances), programmatically verify that extracted line items sum to the stated total to detect extraction errors.

items.label: Maintain extraction accuracy benchmarks with quarterly regression testing
items.description: Establish a labeled ground-truth dataset of 500+ documents per type and run automated accuracy benchmarks quarterly, with a defined minimum accuracy threshold (e.g., 99.5% for monetary fields) before production deployment.

items.label: Log all schema validation failures with document ID and failure reason
items.description: Persist structured logs of every validation failure including document identifier, field name, expected type/value, and actual output to support root cause analysis and audit evidence.

sections.heading: Model Governance & Explainability
sections.description: Ensure AI extraction models meet SOX, SEC, and credit-decision explainability requirements, with documented model lineage, change controls, and human oversight mechanisms for decisions with regulatory consequences.

items.label: Document model card for each LLM used in extraction pipelines
items.description: Maintain a model card specifying the model provider, version, training data provenance, known limitations, and intended use case to satisfy SOX internal control documentation and SEC model risk management expectations.

items.label: Implement model change management with approval gates
items.description: Require formal change approval (including compliance and risk sign-off) before updating the LLM model version or prompt templates used in production extraction pipelines, with rollback capability.

items.label: Provide field-level extraction rationale for credit-relevant data
items.description: For any extracted fields used in credit decisioning (e.g., income, debt obligations), log the source text span that produced each extracted value to satisfy ECOA/Regulation B adverse action explanation requirements.

items.label: Enforce human-in-the-loop review for high-value transaction extractions
items.description: Route extractions from documents involving transactions above a defined threshold (e.g., $10,000 for BSA/AML CTR relevance) to a human reviewer before the data is used in compliance reporting.

items.label: Track and report model drift metrics monthly
items.description: Monitor extraction accuracy, schema failure rates, and confidence score distributions monthly; escalate to model risk management if drift exceeds defined thresholds to maintain SOX control effectiveness.

items.label: Prohibit use of extracted data for financial advice without disclaimers
items.description: Implement application-layer controls ensuring that any output derived from LLM extraction that could constitute financial advice includes SEC-required disclaimers and is reviewed by a licensed professional where required.

items.label: Conduct annual third-party model risk assessment
items.description: Engage an independent model risk reviewer annually to assess the extraction pipeline against SR 11-7 model risk management guidance and document findings for board-level risk reporting.

sections.heading: Access Control, Monitoring & Incident Response
sections.description: Implement least-privilege access, continuous monitoring, and a documented incident response plan tailored to financial data extraction failures, regulatory notification timelines, and customer fund protection obligations.

items.label: Enforce role-based access control with least privilege for extraction pipeline components
items.description: Assign distinct IAM roles to ingestion, extraction, validation, and output components; no single service account should have read-write access across all pipeline stages to limit blast radius of a compromise.

items.label: Enable real-time alerting on anomalous extraction volumes or failure spikes
items.description: Configure monitoring to alert within 15 minutes if extraction failure rates exceed 5% or document ingestion volume deviates more than 3 standard deviations from baseline, indicating potential data pipeline compromise or model failure.

items.label: Define and test a documented incident response runbook for extraction failures
items.description: Maintain a runbook covering containment, evidence preservation, regulatory notification timelines (72-hour GDPR breach notification, SEC Form 8-K material event thresholds), and customer communication procedures.

items.label: Implement automated PII detection and alerting on extraction outputs
items.description: Run extraction outputs through a PII detection layer before persistence; alert and quarantine any output containing unexpected PII fields (e.g., SSNs appearing in non-KYC document extractions) to prevent data leakage.

items.label: Conduct quarterly access reviews for all extraction pipeline service accounts
items.description: Review and recertify all service account permissions quarterly, revoking unused access, to satisfy PCI DSS Requirement 7 and SOX IT general control requirements for access management.

items.label: Maintain SIEM integration for extraction pipeline audit logs
items.description: Forward all extraction pipeline logs (ingestion, validation, output, errors) to the enterprise SIEM with defined correlation rules for detecting unauthorized access patterns or bulk data exfiltration attempts.

items.label: Test incident response plan against extraction failure scenarios semi-annually
items.description: Run tabletop exercises simulating scenarios such as mass extraction failure during quarter-end reporting or LLM provider outage, validating that RTO/RPO targets and regulatory notification timelines can be met.

items.label: Implement data loss prevention (DLP) controls on extraction output destinations
items.description: Apply DLP policies to all systems receiving extracted financial data to prevent unauthorized copying, emailing, or exfiltration of structured financial records, satisfying PCI DSS Requirement 12.3 and GDPR Article 32.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: PCI DSS Requirement 3.3 prohibits storing sensitive authentication data (CVV, full magnetic stripe) post-authorization; ensure LLM extraction pipelines tokenize or redact this data before any model context window and that extracted outputs are scanned to confirm no SAD persists in structured output fields.

BSA/AML obligations require that AI-extracted transaction data used to populate SAR or CTR filings maintains a complete, auditable chain of custody from source document to filed report; any extraction error affecting a SAR filing can constitute a willful violation under 31 U.S.C. § 5318, carrying penalties up to $1M per violation.

GDPR Article 22 restricts solely automated decision-making with legal or significant effects on EU data subjects; if extracted financial data feeds automated credit or fraud decisions, implement a documented human review mechanism and honor data subject rights to explanation and contest under Articles 13–15.

SOX Section 404 requires management to assess the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting; AI extraction pipelines that feed financial statements must be documented as IT general controls with evidence of testing, including schema validation logs, accuracy benchmarks, and change management records, available for external auditor review.

cta.heading: Automate Your Fintech Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your fintech extraction agents against SOX, PCI DSS, and GDPR controls—catching schema failures, access anomalies, and regulatory drift before they become enforcement actions.
